model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current micro-ResNet is likely under-optimized within a fixed 256-step budget. Increasing Adam learning rate from 5e-4 to 1e-3 is a minimal, high-leverage change that often improves early progress on CIFAR-10 without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Activate the already-defined cosine schedule so learning rate decays across the fixed training horizon, which can improve final validation loss at the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay over 256 steps hurt validation loss in the previous run, so disabling the schedule restores the stronger constant-LR behavior.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, slightly weaker L2 regularization can improve fit speed and reduce underfitting while keeping training stable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is very small for CIFAR-10; increasing base channels should raise representational capacity with a simple change, which can improve validation loss under a fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous capacity increase improved loss, so the next simplest extension is another moderate width bump to further improve representation quality within the same training budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"insert_after","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","text":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After widening the network, adding one more residual block is a targeted capacity increase that should improve feature refinement with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the widened/deeper model now performing well, switching to AdamW can improve optimization stability and regularization behavior versus Adam at the same learning-rate scale and short budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After moving to AdamW with higher-capacity architecture, a slightly lower learning rate can reduce overshooting and improve final validation loss within the same fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lower learning rate degraded validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the better optimization speed observed with the current architecture and AdamW.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With increased model capacity and extra residual depth, a modestly stronger weight decay may improve generalization and lower validation loss while keeping optimization behavior close to the current best.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher weight decay degraded validation loss, so reverting to 5e-5 restores the stronger regularization setting for the current AdamW architecture.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The backbone capacity has increased, so enlarging the classifier hidden layer is a simple matching capacity adjustment that may capture richer class-separating features at the output stage.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing classifier width hurt validation loss, so reverting to the smaller head restores the stronger, simpler configuration.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"A small amount of label smoothing can improve generalization and calibration with minimal complexity, especially in short-budget training where overconfident fits can hurt validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing worsened validation loss in this fixed-budget setting, so reverting to plain cross-entropy restores the stronger baseline behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Given the stronger architecture and no scheduler, a modest LR increase may improve progress per step within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate significantly worsened validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the strongest observed optimization setting.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed step budget, doubling batch size exposes the model to more examples per optimizer step and may improve validation loss without architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
